methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Bellatrix Aerospace is a pre-Series B stage space and aerospace company that has raised $8M to date. The valuation landscape for this sector comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

A peer-median approach is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow pre-Series B cohort in Space & Aerospace; while 23 same-sector peers exist broadly, none of the granular stage and valuation filters yielded three or more datapoints necessary for robust triangulation. Public-comp anchoring via the sector's median P/S multiple of 49.8x across the six public tickers cannot be applied because Bellatrix's revenue is not on file, consistent with typical private company disclosure practices.

Without applicable quantitative triangulation methods, a defensible valuation range cannot be derived from this dataset. Resolution would require either (1) identification of revenue figures for the target, enabling public-comp anchoring, or (2) access to pricing data from at least three pre-Series B or adjacent-stage Space & Aerospace private transactions. The key uncertainty is whether comparable funding rounds in this subsector exist with disclosed post-money valuations.
